purpose: Identifies the dominant macro stress source and predicts credit market behavior and portfolio correlation dynamics based on VIX-MOVE correlation regime.
components: Regime 1 (Strong positive ~0.65): Demand shock — both vol measures elevated, credit spreads widen fastest, funding channel activated
Regime 2 (Decoupled ~0.25-0.35): Supply/inflation shock — MOVE elevated, VIX suppressed, rates-led stress
Regime 3 (Predicted — VIX up, MOVE down): Fiscal tightening plus levered private sector — early warning signal, untested
Regime 4 (Weakly positive +0.20-0.35 — current): Historically most unstable transitional regime
current_assessment: Currently in the weakly-positive regime (+0.20 to +0.35), historically the most unstable. Specific transition probabilities from this state are unreliable (overfit to n=4 sample). The qualitative insight that this is unstable and transitional is valuable even without precise probabilities.
limitations: Transition probability estimates are overfit (n=4 episodes)
Credit-specific diagnostic use requires additional filters
Causal chain from macro shock type to correlation regime is clean theoretically but noisy empirically
